item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I realy like this

But i have one gripe Ioannes is the GREEK variation of John (technically its the apposite but whatever) NOT Latin

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And then the CotBG and Sarkic cults said:

"Yeah, no, not that."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do not see the coming apocalypse in this scenario as being cause either by Folowers of Mekhane of the Sarktics.
This guy in the seal right there….
That shit is third party that just want to mess with both followers of Mekhane and Sarctics.
Because as its believed, once it devour the parts of the duality, he ascend to godhood and most likely reshape all of reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome addition to the Sarkik and Mekhanite lore. A new vision of the potential apocalypse caused by these two deities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, Ion was much further in the past during Ancient China. He destabilized the Daevites and left them open to invasion from Ancient China. That is how I understand it, at least. Also, Ion ascended before the Proto-Sarkic/Mekhanite war which was in Ancient Greece. So yeah, this isn't Ion, just somebody else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, I share the sentiment, but the earliest date mentioned is the 5th century AD. I mostly get annoyed when people put Sarkicism earlier than the Gnostic religions it borrows terminology from, 2nd or 3rd century AD. (I'm happy to accept Mekhanites further back within recorded history because it's fun.)

This one is very well integrated, IMO.

I kind of agree with the other critique, though. You're relying on the premise of "Sarkicism + CotBG + Christianity (+ spooky cannibalism!)" rather than what's shown to pull a lot of the weight, and it just doesn't pull enough. There was a bit of historical imagery in the window events, but there was so much more that you could have mined to present or at least imply an actual narrative.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always thought it was funny that the founder of Sarkicism was essentially named
"John"
- Hide

Am I right? Is Prester John the Grand Karcist?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always imagine a union of flesh and metal to be a god of wood and a devourer of all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool to see this get featured — it deserved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite liked this skip. Aside from being an irredeemable Sarkic fanboy, I really enjoyed some of the details you put in there, like using "hemomancy" as a scholarly term for fleshcrafting, or that the Foundation actively employs thaumaturges and thaumatologists. These all agree perfectly with my headcanon.

Personally, I prefer to call the language "Adymite" rather than "Adytite"; I find that it rolls off the tongue easier. But it's good enough, IMO, naming it for the empire and using the appropriate Hebrew-derived suffix.

What I liked less about it was the cooperation between the CotBG and the Sarkites, especially seeing as how the Mekhanites hate Sarkicism with a burning passion. In goes against my headcanon, and a lot of other CotBG and Sarkic material. So it's quite a clash that I've a hard time suspending my disbelief for, interesting as it is. Still, the article as a whole outweighs that one complaint on my side.

I think it could do with some additions, maybe flesh out the context a bit more. All we know is that this person is a reality bender, but virtually nothing about what they did, what happened and why they were so bad that two sworn enemies (at least from the Mekhanite perspective) would cooperate with each other to combat them. There's a lot of unexploited potential here, and I'm curious to see more of it.

+1. Because even if I might sound overly negative, here, I still enjoyed this. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love references to Soviet Central Asia and Prester John. Unfortunately, that's not enough to overcome the fact that 1) I don't care at all for the "Sarkic cults/CotBG have existed since eleventy fillion years BC" narrative 2) It's another window to yet another world 3) the story itself is one that fundamentally does not interest me.

Competent for what it is, but I don't really like what it is. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which parts in particular do you think I should expand? I feel what you're saying, but I'm not entirely sure how to go about it. I'm open to suggestions basically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you enjoyed! And I definitely love GOI interactions too, especially when its a relationship unrelated to the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow, damn.

worshippers believe SCP-3221-2 will eventually consume both halves of the duality in order to ascend as God.

I am very afraid of this happening!

People always get excited (I think? Maybe it's just me) to see the Foundation working with GoI's, be they GOC, UIU or ORIA. But this is the first time I have ever seen them working, essentially, with either CotBG or Sarkicism… And then it's both. And they're working together, too. That right there tells you how fucked-up and dangerous this guy is. Excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glorious :D.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I adore this beyond words.

(also impressed you made it that quickly!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding here. After finishing the article I felt like I missed out something but after scouring the article, nothing came up. As much as I adore the idea, I really think it would really benefit from expansion…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry but I just have to:

+ GOIball Version
- GOIball Version

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To reiterate what I told you on IRC, the SCP feels like it needs to be expanded. The imagery in the observation log is intriguing, but weak and underdeveloped, as it fails to paint an actual world, rather than just several disjointed scenes. The para-religious and para-historical aspects are also rather interesting, but they don't meld together quite right. I understood the end result, but it didn't feel as interesting or as memorable as it could be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you enjoyed!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You really know that whatever is locked in the box should probably stay in the box if the Sarkic Cults, CotBG, and ORIA all chipped in for the lock.

I find the build up here to be wonderful, and the imagery presented in the Window event observations is quite visceral.

+1.

item.title: Author's Note
item.content:encoded:
This story was the result of an unconventional collaboration between myself and Tufto. Originally, this was an ORIA piece written solely by him. After he confided in me that he wasn't happy with how it was going, I offered to collab with him and offered some ideas on how to do it. He decided he didn't want to continue working on the project, but gave me his blessing to continue work on the original draft. While almost everything has been changed, the majority of the visual log and nearly the entire prayer were originally his.

Given his recent decision to quit the site, I dedicate this piece to him. He's back! :P

Thanks to Metaphysician, Conwell, Ghostchibi, ARD, Weryllium, SunnyClockwork, and Zyn for critique!

+ Image attribution
- Hide

https://www.flickr.com/photos/wwarby/11271766325
CC by SA 2.0

If you enjoyed, check out my other articles here.
